label: Wine
note: oinops = oinos (wine) + ops (eye/face). Attested 17x in Homer (5 Iliad, 12 Odyssey), always of the sea — and, oddly, of oxen.

label: Burgundy
note: Homer's wine is only ever red or black; white wine seems absent from his world.

label: Dusk
note: Gladstone (1858) noted Homer's near-total absence of 'blue' and read the color words as luminance, not hue.

label: Indigo
note: kyanos describes the eyebrows of Zeus and there means dark, not blue.

label: Slate
note: One theory: the sea genuinely looks dark red / slate at certain light.

label: Deep
note: The palette the story reads in — dark sea under stars, parchment for the text.
